mechanism: Compressed ROIC-WACC spreads (~4-5pp vs ~8-10pp in 2020-21) create convex rate sensitivity — further rate increases destroy a disproportionately larger share of economic profit. Current equity valuations (~21x) do not fully reflect this amplified rate sensitivity under a positive correlation regime.
regime_dependence: The convexity effect intensifies as the ROIC-WACC spread narrows further. At very wide spreads (>8pp), rate sensitivity is approximately linear. The relationship is strongest for companies with low ROIC relative to cost of capital.
